properties.publicTransport: Free shuttle bus services will operate for the game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip
properties.otherAdvice:
FIFA Women's Series - Matildas v Mexico at McDonald Jones Stadium. Kick-off at 7.15pm, full time around 9.15pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Griffiths Road

properties.publicTransport: Buses replace trains from Cronulla to Sutherland. Match tickets include travel on trains. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sharks v Dragons at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. NSW Cup from 1.40pm. NRL kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 6pm.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd.

Captain Cook Drive will be closed in the immediate vicinity of the Stadium from 15-minutes before full-time until 30-minutes after full time (45-minute closure). 

All road closures will be implemented and managed by NSW Police. Allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

roads.mainStreet: Captain Cook Drive

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
AFL: Sydney Swans v St Kilda at the SCG. First bounce at 3.15pm, full time at 5.55pm.

With up to 35,000 footy fans attending, significant traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park.

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Attendees are urged to catch public transport and leave the car at home to avoid significant traffic delays and the high demand for parking.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 11.15am and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car parks open from 12.15pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 11am to 8pm on sections of Albion Street, Moore Park Road, Fitzroy Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 8pm on sections of Cleveland Street. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Wests Tigers v Panthers at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. Kick-off at 2pm, full time around 3.45pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 3.45pm until 4.15pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Rabbitohs v Broncos at Accor Stadium in Olympic Park. Kick-off at 7.50pm, full time around 9.35pm.

Traffic will be heavy as fans travel to and from the match. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

properties.otherAdvice:
IVE at Qudos Bank Arena on Saturday 13 June. Show starts at 7.30pm

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you're driving, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

NRL: WestsTigers v Titans at Leichhardt Oval. Kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 6pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on James St, Perry St, Balmain Rd, Mary St and Glover St as attendees travel to and from the match.

A section of Mary Street may close before the game and will reopen when crowds have cleared the area. Traffic will be detoured via Chapel St while the closure is in place.   

Motorists needing access to other venues in the area should use either Maliyawul St or Glover St.

Attendees driving to the game should allow extra travel time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

Friday 12 June - After Dark event and street party - traffic changes and parking restrictions

  • From 5pm Fri 12 June to 4am Sat 13 Jun - Full road closure on Robert St between Mullens St and Port Access Rd
  • 8am Temporary no parking zone on southern side of Robert St
  • 4pm Temporary no parking zone on northern side of Robert St

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Interactive road closure and clearway map - Parramatta Half Marathon

properties.publicTransport: Light rail services will not run between Parramatta Square and Westmead from 5am to 12pm. Route 545 buses will be diverting and using different stops while roads are closed. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Up to 8,000 people are expected to take part in the inaugural Parramatta Half Marathon on Sunday 21 June starting from 7am. Participants will begin their run on O'Connell St bridge and continue through Parramatta Park, around Parramatta CBD before finishing at CommBank Stadium by 11.15am.

Road closures will be in place to support the event.

  • 3am to 10.30am: roads in Western Sydney Uni campus area including sections of Fifth St and Railway St, and in Parramatta on River Rd West between James Ruse Dr and Alfred St.
  • 3am to 11am: roads in Parramatta CBD including sections of Marsden St, George St, Thomas St, Phillip St, Macarthur St, Palmer St, Marist Pl, Market St, Sorrell St, Elizabeth St and surrounding local roads. Access for residents will be maintained on sections of Phillip St, George St and surrounding local lanes.
  • 3am to 11am: all roads in Parramatta Park west of Parramatta River will be closed.
  • 3am to 12 midday: roads in Parramatta CBD including O'Connell St between Victoria Rd and Argyle St, and sections of Park Pde, Alexandra Ave eastbound, Macquarie, George, Hunter and Pitt Streets, and roads in Parramatta Park east of Parramatta River.
  • The Valley Cycleway (Parramatta River foreshore shared path) will be closed to cyclists and pedestrians between 6:30am and 10:30am during the event period.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on roads in the area.

Special event clearways will be in effect between 1am and 12pm midday on sections of O'Connell St, Victoria Rd, Elizabeth St, Hunter St, Macquarie St, George St, Macarthur St, River Rd West and other local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator

properties.diversions:
Motorists travelling north or south of Parramatta CBD will be detoured via Wilde Ave, Smith St and Station St East only. All other key roads corridors will be closed for the event.

Access to Parramatta Westfield will remain available from the south.

Detour around Parramatta CBD via James Ruse Dr, Victoria Rd, Cumberland Hwy, and Great Western Hwy to avoid creating and being caught in traffic congestion and delays.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be set up for the FIFA World Cup men's games from Sunday 14 to Friday 26 June.

The matches will be shown live on the big screen and the site is free to visit:

  • Sunday 14 June: Australia vs Türkiye, kick off 2pm AEST
  • Saturday 20 June: USA v Australia, kick off 5am AEST
  • Friday 26 June: Paraguay v Australia, kick off 12pm AEST

Traffic may be heavier than usual in the area. Motorists should be aware of increased pedestrian activity on roads around Parramatta Square and station.

webLinks.linkText: World Cup live site - Darling Harbour

properties.publicTransport: L1 Light rail services run regularly to Exhibition Centre and Paddys Markets within walking distance of Tumbalong Park. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
A live site will be open at Tumbalong Park in Darling Harbour from Sunday 14 June to Monday 20 July for the upcoming FIFA World Cup Men's matches.

The matches will be shown live on the big screen and the site is free to visit:

  • Sunday 14 June: Australia vs Türkiye, kick off 2pm AEST
  • Saturday 20 June: USA v Australia, kick off 5am AEST
  • Friday 26 June: Paraguay v Australia, kick off 12pm AEST
  • 9 – 11 July: Quarter-finals, date and time TBC
  • 14 – 15 July: Semi-finals, date and time TBC
  • Mon 20 July: Final, time TBC

Attendees are encouraged to catch public transport as traffic may be heavier than usual.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads throughout the Sydney CBD.

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Vivid Sydney - top tips to plan your trip

properties.publicTransport: Trains are not dropping off at Circular Quay from 6pm-11pm, pick up only. Buses that usually run to Circular Quay are using stops at Martin Place instead until midnight. Light rail is not running between Circular Quay and Town Hall until last service. For more details and top tips to plan your trip, visit transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Vivid Sydney returns to light up the city for its 16th year across 23 consecutive nights from Friday 22 May to Saturday 13 June. Drone shows have been cancelled for the remainder of Vivid, visit the website for details.

The city is no place for cars with road closures and clearway parking restrictions in place each night in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Dawes Point and Millers Point between 3pm and midnight daily, and until 1am on Saturday nights.

Road closures - Saturdays:

  • *CLOSED* From 3pm local roads in The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point including sections of High St, High Ln, Lower Fort St, Argyle Place and Circular Quay West.
  • *CLOSED* From 5pm sections of George St, Argyle St, Hickson Rd, Essex St, Pottinger St, Merriman St, Rodens Ln, Upper Fort St and Watson Rd. Roads in Circular Quay including Pitt, Loftus, Young, Albert and Phillip Streets are closed.
  • Additionally Grosvenor St/Bridge St between York St and Loftus St is closed, including the Grosvenor St off-ramp from the Harbour Bridge - no east/west access via Bridge St during this time. Roads remain closed until 1am.
  • From 7pm: Wheat Rd will close until 11pm.

Special event clearways are in effect on most major roads in the city between 3pm and midnight. Some clearways have been in effect earlier from 1pm and 2pm on local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: George St, Hickson Rd, Bridge St
roads.suburb: Circular Quay, The Rocks, Barangaroo, Darling Harbour

Bob Charley AO Stakes Day at Royal Randwick Racecourse. First race from 11am, last race from 4.20pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on Anzac Pde, Doncaster Ave, High St, Alison Rd and Darley Rd as crowds travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

roads.mainStreet: Anzac Parade, Alison Road, High Street

properties.otherAdvice:
Road closures will be in place on Saturday 6 June 2026 for the Merriwa Festival of the Fleeces.

The Golden Highway (Bettington Street) will be closed in both directions from 05:00am - 5:00pm.

Motorists travelling should allow extra travel time and expect delays.
properties.diversions: A detour will be in place via Marquet Street and Blaxland Street.
